The negation operator disables any possi: The negation operator disables any possible output of a function. This can be useful for functions that return a "status" after execution, such as booleans or numbers.
The silent operator will suppress any er: The silent operator will suppress any error a function might throw and stops further code execution as well.
The count operator directly counts the v: The count operator directly counts the values of a possible array output from a function using a delimiter (separator). This operator only takes in **1 character**.
